title: Add create_schematic operation
description: The kicad-agent has no operation to create a new .kicad_sch file. All 19+ operations require an existing target file. Add a create_schematic op that generates a valid empty KiCad schematic with proper header, UUID, paper size, and title block. Also consider create_pcb and create_project variants. Schema is in src/kicad_agent/ops/schema.py, executor in src/kicad_agent/ops/executor.py.

title: Add create_symbol operation for new component definitions
description: The kicad-agent has no operation to create new component symbols in .kicad_sym library files. AddLibEntryOp only adds library references to sym-lib-table. Need a create_symbol op that defines a symbol with name, pins (number, name, type, position), graphic elements, and properties (reference, value, footprint, datasheet). This is needed to add new ICs like the AK4619VN codec that don't exist in standard or project libraries.

title: Document all operations in prompt.md - 12+ missing
description: prompt.md only documents 19 operations but the schema has 31+. Missing: AddWireOp, AddLabelOp, AddPowerOp, AddJunctionOp, AddNoConnectOp, AddNetClassOp, AssignNetClassOp, AddCopperZoneOp, AddDesignRuleOp, AutoRouteOp, RepairSchematicOp, SetBoardOutlineOp, ValidatePowerNetsOp. These all work in the schema but are not documented for LLM consumption.

title: Add component search and symbol import MCP server
description: kicad-agent needs a way to import component symbols from external sources (SnapMagic, Ultra Librarian, LCSC, Octopart). Currently there's no create_symbol operation and no way to fetch existing symbols from part databases. Build an MCP server that: (1) searches for parts by name/MPN, (2) downloads KiCad symbol + footprint into project library, (3) returns pinout/spec data. This would eliminate the manual symbol creation workaround and speed up schematic design across all 22+ modules. Priority sources: SnapMagic (free KiCad symbols), LCSC (already sourcing from), Octopart (aggregator API).

title: Manually created AK4619VN symbol - workaround for missing create_symbol op
description: Workaround: manually created AK4619VN KiCad symbol in shared library because kicad-agent has no create_symbol operation (kicad-agent-2) and no component import MCP (kicad-agent-4). Also manually created pair-card.kicad_sch because no create_schematic operation (kicad-agent-1). Future: automate via create_symbol op + SnapMagic/LCSC MCP.

title: Add update_pcb_from_schematic operation
description:
CRITICAL GAP: kicad-agent has no operation to sync footprints and net assignments from hierarchical schematic into PCB. This is the equivalent of KiCad GUI Tools > Update PCB from Schematic, which is the PRIMARY gateway operation for PCB layout.

WHAT IT DOES:
1. Parse hierarchical schematic root (.kicad_sch) and all sub-sheets
2. Export netlist via kicad-cli sch export netlist
3. For each symbol instance: find matching PCB footprint by ref designator
   - If match: update pad-to-net assignments from netlist
   - If missing from PCB: add new footprint with correct pads + nets
   - If in PCB but not schematic: mark as orphan (optional removal)
4. Preserve existing PCB placement positions for matched footprints
5. Handle embedded footprints (lib_symbols in schematic) vs library footprints
6. Respect fp-lib-table library paths for footprint resolution

WHY CRITICAL: Without this, any schematic edit that adds/removes components or changes net connections cannot be propagated to the PCB. This blocks the entire autonomous PCB workflow after schematic design is complete. Currently agents must either ask humans to open KiCad GUI or write fragile Python scripts with kiutils (known serialization corruption risk).

INPUTS: schematic_path (root .kicad_sch), pcb_path (.kicad_pcb), fp_lib_table_path (optional)
OUTPUTS: Updated .kicad_pcb + change report (added/updated/orphaned footprints, net changes)

SCHEMA LOCATION: src/kicad_agent/ops/_schema_crossfile.py (cross-file op, like PropagateSymbolChangeOp)
HANDLER LOCATION: src/kicad_agent/ops/handlers/ (new file or extend crossfile handler)

APPROACH:
- Use kicad-cli sch export netlist to get schematic components + nets
- Parse KiCad netlist format (comp/footprint/net nodes)
- Load PCB via kiutils Board
- Match footprints by reference designator
- For matched: compare pad nets, update mismatches
- For unmatched (in schematic, not PCB): create FootprintItem with pad definitions from KiCad library or schematic embedded lib_symbols
- For orphans (in PCB, not schematic): flag for review
- Atomic: backup PCB before modification, restore on failure

CAVEATS:
- kiutils Board serialization can corrupt PCBs (known issue from analog-ecosystem Phase 24)
- Must handle KiCad 10 embedded footprints (full S-expression in lib_symbols section)
- Must preserve footprint positions and orientations
- Net class assignments in .kicad_pro should NOT be overwritten (they're separate from PCB S-expression net classes)

title: Enhance auto_route with netlist-aware multi-layer production routing
description:
AutoRouteOp exists (kicad-agent/src/kicad_agent/ops/_schema_pcb.py) with A* pathfinding, multi-layer support, impedance control, and length matching. However it needs enhancement for production-quality PCB routing.

CURRENT CAPABILITIES (verified in schema):
- Single-layer and multi-layer routing
- Impedance target calculation
- Length matching for diff pairs (sawtooth pattern)
- single_pass and multi_pass strategies
- Net-specific routing (filter by net name)

NEEDED ENHANCEMENTS:
1. Net class compliance: Route traces with correct widths per net class definition (Power=0.5mm, Audio=0.3mm, HighSpeed=0.2mm, etc.) -- read from .kicad_pro net_settings.classes
2. Differential pair support: Proper diff pair routing (not just length matching) -- coupled traces with controlled gap for USB 90-ohm and Ethernet 100-ohm
3. Via placement: Automatic via placement at layer transitions with correct via diameter/drill per net class
4. Obstacle avoidance: Route around placed components, pads, existing traces, keep-out zones
5. Rip-up and retry: multi_pass already exists but needs to work with actual DRC violations (not just grid hits)
6. Pour-zone awareness: Avoid routing over filled zone areas, or route in ways that don't break zone connectivity
7. Design rule compliance: Respect clearance rules from .kicad_pro design_settings.rules
8. TDM length matching: Match bus widths (BCLK/LRCK/SDIN/SDOUT) within tolerance for codec-to-B2B paths

WHY: Phase 21.5 Plan 04 needs full board routing on both analog and digital boards. Without production-quality auto_route, the only option is human KiCad GUI interaction. The current A* pathfinder is a good foundation but needs DRC integration and net class awareness.

PRIORITY: Lower than kicad-agent-6 (update_pcb_from_schematic is a hard blocker), but needed before Plan 04 can complete autonomously.

RELATED ISSUES: kicad-agent-6 (update_pcb_from_schematic must run before auto_route)

title: Intelligent auto-route with obstacle awareness — Phase A unblocks channel strip
description: auto_route operation has full infrastructure (A*, multi-layer, impedance, length matching, S-expression bridge) but produces 1.5% success rate because no obstacle data is passed to the routing graph. Needs: (1) extract footprint bounding boxes as obstacles, (2) pad-to-grid snapping, (3) power net filtering, (4) priority-based sequential routing with rip-up. Full spec: docs/FEATURE-007-intelligent-auto-route.md
